mnemonics.title: Business Case Components
mnemonics.content: P-MART-S: Problem, Market analysis, Analysis, Recommendations, Timeline, Success metrics

mnemonics.title: Design Thinking Stages
mnemonics.content: E-DI-P-T: Empathize, Define, Ideate, Prototype, Test (remember: 'Every Designer Invents Playful Things')

mnemonics.title: UX Maturity Levels
mnemonics.content: A-D-S-I-S: Ad-hoc, Developing, Strategic, Integrated, Sustainable

acronyms.usage: Financial gain from UX investment relative to cost
Financial losses from bad user experience
Customer loyalty metric based on recommendation likelihood
Overall experience of a user with a product or service
Organization and structure of content in a product

visualTips.visualization: Think of a pyramid: Top = Recommendation, Middle = Analysis (costs/benefits), Bottom = Problem Statement - building from foundation to conclusion
Think of a staircase with 5 steps: Step 1 (Ad-hoc) → Step 5 (Strategic), each step higher means more organizational integration
Formula as a balance scale: Benefits on one side, Costs on the other. The more benefits outweigh costs, the higher the ROI

analogies.analogy: Like a movie pitch - you need to convince producers (stakeholders) that your idea will make money (ROI) before they fund it
Like a leaky pipe - each drip represents lost customers, support calls, and revenue - the leak seems small but costs millions over time
Like a car's dashboard - different gauges (speed, fuel, temperature) tell you different things about performance, you need multiple metrics to understand UX health
Like cooking - Empathize (taste preferences), Define (recipe), Ideate (brainstorm dishes), Prototype (sample dish), Test (feedback)
